In this case, SAP recommends merging data from the different SAP Solution Manager systems into one SAP Cloud ALM tenant, once the selective data transfer for solution documentation is available. It is not recommended to consolidate the SAP Solution Manager systems beforehand, as this implies huge efforts and little benefits.

In the future, SAP plans to offer the possibility to purchase more than one SAP Cloud ALM tenant per customer number.

 

Conclusion

The scope of your transition to SAP Cloud ALM depends on your actual ALM footprint and should be planned alongside that. Get a clear understanding of what you are doing today in SAP Solution Manager, what the equivalent capabilities are in SAP Cloud ALM (and don’t forget to look into SAP Focused Run if you have advanced needs for monitoring and operations!), and then plan your transition accordingly.

You can find all the information about the transition - from functional comparisons to planning aspects and customer examples – in the Transition Center on the SAP Support Portal.

Let me know in the comments how far you are in your transition to SAP Cloud ALM!

 

#cloudalm #solman #focusedrun #transition

entry.title: SAP Maintenance Resource Scheduling Part -1 ( With apps and Step By Step guide)
entry.content:
Use Case:

This sprawling text gives you the crisp idea to maintain one’s available resource capacities in their optimum capacity. Also provides Information regarding a different scenario for resource scheduling, through Various available functionalities.

  • Order operations can be scheduled based on the priority/criticality of the work.
  • Schedules can be created for a target week by selecting operations from multiple maintenance orders.
  • Refinement of the schedules can be done based on the utilization forecast of the work centers for the week.

Prema facially to take advantage of Maintenance Resource Schedule through Fiori launchpad following apps are needs to be adopted in system with giving appropriate roles and authorization to authenticated End-User.  

  1. Resource Scheduling for Maintenance Planners (App ID: F2227).
  2. Manage Schedule (App ID: F3326).
  3. Maintenance Scheduling Board (App ID: F2603).
  4. Assign Maintenance Order Operation (App ID: F3423).

In the upcoming para will be dealing section wise functionalities in Maintenance Resource Schedule through Fiori launchpad Apps. Such as assign work center, create Work Center Team, create planning bucket, manage schedule, Freeze Schedule.

Apps look like as,

A. Assign Work Centre:

  1. To assign work Centre, Access the Resource Scheduling for Maintenance Planners (F2227) app.
  2. Choose the user profile button on the top right-hand corner of the screen.
  3. Choose My Work Centers.

4.Add a row (+) and enter the name of the work center that you want to assign to yourself.
5.Select the right work center from the list of proposed work centers
6.Choose Assign.


You can also change capacities likewise:

B. Manage Schedule:

B.1. Create Schedule:

  1. Choose the Resource Scheduling for Maintenance Planners tile.
  2. Enter the required filter values and choose the Go button.
  3. Choose the header area of Work Center Utilization card.
  4. Select due order operations by selecting the checkbox.
  5. Choose Add to Schedule. A dialog box opens.

  1. Select Create New Schedule and enter a schedule name and the time period (define timeframe for the schedule).
  2. Choose Create and Open Schedule. 

    Create New Schedule: Test Schedule

In the similar way new schedule will display on screen.

B.2. Level Work Centre Utilization

  1. Check the weekly utilization forecast on the List View tab.
  2. If work center utilization is not yet optimal, use one of the following options:

Add more due order operations. To do so, use the back button and follow these steps:

  • Select a few more due order operations by selecting the checkbox.
  • Choose Add to Schedule. A dialog box opens.
  • Select the radio button Add to Existing Schedule and then select the schedule created in previous step.
  • Choose Add and Open Schedule.

Remove order operations from the schedule if there's not enough capacity to complete them in the target week. To do so, follow these steps on the Manage Schedule page:

  • Select the operation on the List View tab.
  • Choose the three dots and choose the Remove button.

3. To move order operations to another work center, select the operation by marking the checkbox and choose the required work center from the Change Work Center dropdown list.

B.3. Refine Schedule

  1. In the Manage Schedules (F3326) app, choose Refine Schedule to start planning at day level.
  2. Select the order operations whose start day you want to set by marking the checkbox.
  3. Schedule the selected order operations by using one of the following options:

Choose Schedule Automatically.

The app finds a start day and time slot for each order operation.

An information popup is displayed. Select on Do not show again and choose OK.

  1. Choose Set Start Date and Time.

B.4. Dispatch Order Operations

  1. Choose the arrow on the Freeze Schedule button.
  2. Choose Save and Dispatch Operations. The app tries to dispatch all scheduled operations.

B.5. Cancel the Dispatch OR Change Start Date of Dispatched Operations.

  1. Open the Resource Scheduling for Maintenance Planners (F2227) app.
  2. Choose the header area of the Work Center Utilization card.
  3. The Manage Work Center Utilization app opens.
  4. Filter the list of order operations for the order operations whose dispatch you want to cancel.
  5. Select the relevant order operations by marking the checkbox.
  6. Choose Cancel Dispatch.
  7. Select the relevant order operations by marking the checkbox.
  8. Choose Change.
  9. Enter a new start date (and, if required, time).
  10. To immediately dispatch the operation, select the relevant value in the Trigger or Cancel Dispatch field.
  11. Choose Change.

entry.title: SAP Academic Community Conference Armenia
entry.content:
The SAP academic community cordially invites you to participate in “The Academic Integration Approaches to SAP Solutions” conference held on 22 February, 2024, in the Center for Digital Transformation and Data Analyses at the Armenian State University of Armenia (ASUE). The agenda includes welcome remarks by ASUE Rector Diana Galoyan, SAP University Alliance Partners Director Michael Nurenberg and Coordinator for the Center for Digital Transformation and Data Analyses Sos Khachikyan, as well as presentations by business representatives and academic partners.

🖥️ Where?  Faculty of Computer Science and Statistics, Center for Digital Transformation and Data Analyses

🗺️ Address: 77 P. Sevak Street, 0069 Yerevan, Armenia

🔗 Link: https://events.sap.com/sap-acc-global-website/en/home
